_doc: PACKAGING LEDGER (self-maintaining). Mirrors the finished-goods inventory model: each component carries a COUNT ANCHOR (the only hand-entered on-hand), plus RECEIPT events (landed POs) and — via consumption_log + run_calendar — CONSUMPTION events (production runs). engine/packaging_plan.js COMPUTES live on-hand = anchor.qty + receipts-since − consumption-since. NEVER hand-edit a computed on-hand; only (a) add a receipt when a PO lands, (b) add a run to consumption_log when it produces, or (c) replace a component anchor when a fresh physical count arrives (reconcile drift first).
_writer: human maintains anchors/receipts/consumption_log; engine/packaging_plan.js reads, writes only spine/packaging_status.json
source: RE-ANCHORED 2026-07-20 to the FK weekly physical count dated 2026-07-17 (scanned inventory sheet, Cotto/Dipsy, received with the 7/13-7/15 batch sheets). This is the fresh count the prior interim anchor was waiting on. Every tub/lid/label anchor below is now FK's counted 7/17 figure, so all receipts dated on/before 7/17 (Cary SO680747 15k tubs+lids ~7/1; ATL PO070126 lid_fo 6k / wrap_fo 3k / wrap_gr 3k ~7/9) are BAKED INTO the count and have been removed to avoid double-counting. The 7/13 run in consumption_log likewise predates the anchor and is excluded by the engine automatically (kept as history). BOXES were NOT on FK's 7/17 sheet, so box_6pk keeps its 5/21 anchor + the 7/14 PCA receipt and remains ROUGH.
_drift_at_reanchor: Ledger-computed vs FK-counted at re-anchor (computed − counted): tubs 9,980 vs 10,039 (+59, excellent); lids 28,610 vs 9,698 (-18,912 — SEE BELOW, resolved); wrap_buf 4,257 vs 1,800 (-2,457, the worst real drift — Buffalo wrap was being consumed faster than logged); wrap_fo 3,368 vs 4,000 (+632); wrap_gr 2,568 vs 3,700 (+1,132); lid_buf 1,957 vs 2,300 (+343); lid_fo 4,468 vs 4,100 (-368); lid_gr 3,768 vs 4,050 (+282). Label drift is consistent with the 3% scrap factor being approximate plus partial-sheet waste; tubs prove the ledger mechanics are sound.
_lids_ambiguity_RESOLVED: The 6/26 anchor carried an unresolved read: 21,675 (FK scan) vs 2,675 (old spine). The 7/17 count settles it — the OLD SPINE WAS RIGHT and 21,675 was a misread of 2,675. Proof: from 2,675, the ledger computes 2,675 + 15,000 receipts - 8,065 consumed = 9,610, versus FK's counted 9,698 (drift +88). From 21,675 it computes 28,610, which is ~3x the physical shelf. CONSEQUENCE: lids are NOT the comfortable position the ledger showed — real on-hand is ~9.7k against ~18.7k of 90-day consumption, so lids now need reordering rather than sitting GREEN.

consumption.note: yields of record are 75/63/67 (run actuals); label scrap 3%

consumption_log.basis: batch sheet (7/13-7/15 run: FO 1+4+1 = 6 batches, GR 3, BUF 3 — confirmed against PRO-FM-02 logs). PREDATES the 7/17 anchor, so it is history only and is not re-consumed.

anchor.source: FK weekly count 7/17 (unlabeled cups). Ledger had computed 9,980 — drift +59.
FK weekly count 7/17. RESOLVES the 21,675-vs-2,675 ambiguity in favour of 2,675 (see _lids_ambiguity_RESOLVED). Lids are ~9.7k, not ~28.6k.
FK weekly count 7/17 (Buffalo labels - cups). Ledger had 4,257 — drift -2,457, the largest real gap.

anchor.source: FK 5/21 - boxes omitted from BOTH the 6/26 and 7/17 counts, so this stays ROUGH. Ask Nathan to add boxes to the weekly sheet.

_prelabeled_wip: FK's 7/17 sheet also counted PRE-LABELED cups (tubs already wrapped, staged to fill): Buffalo 23, French Onion 0, Garden Ranch 70 = 93 units. Immaterial (<0.1% of tub stock) and already-committed WIP, so it is NOT added to any anchor — recorded here so a future count that folds them in does not read as drift.
leadDaysNote: leadDays from ACTUAL PO->delivery scrape: ATL labels ~8-10d (was a ~21d scramble early w/ rush fees, now reliable ~5-7d ship + 1d CO Ground); Cary lids ~7d reliable, TUBS ~7d IN STOCK but real backorder risk (May 34WRC8 went ~4wk) so budgeted 14d; PCA boxes ~14d repeat. Boxes anchor is the 5/21 count so box on-hand is ROUGH; consumption from all runs since 5/21.
